description: Organic chemistry - mechanisms, reactions, stereochemistry, synthesis.

carbanion intermediate with base attack first

facts.context: E2 elimination requires the leaving group and beta-hydrogen to be:
distractors: syn-periplanar on the same side of molecule
at any arbitrary angle relative to each other
perpendicular at exactly 90 degrees apart

facts.context: Grignard reagents react with water to produce:

no reaction since Grignards are water-stable
alkene through elimination of magnesium

facts.truth: carbocation rearrangement to more stable form
distractors: no rearrangements occur during the reaction
reaction only works on deactivated arenes
no Lewis acid catalyst is ever required

facts.context: Aldol condensation product after dehydration is:

ether from carbonyl oxygen coupling
carboxylic acid from oxidation
stepwise radical chain mechanism throughout
ionic two-step through zwitterion intermediate
photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition only

facts.context: A molecule is chiral when it has:

at least two stereocenters required minimum
planar geometry with pi bond system
direction of optical rotation measured
alphabetical order of substituent names
molecular weight of attached groups
permanently modify functional groups in product
catalyze the desired reaction transformation
are never removed after the synthesis
forward planning from starting materials only
random trial of possible bond formations
computer-only without chemist input
increasing electronegativity of attacking atom
decreasing size of the nucleophilic species
more electron-withdrawing substituents present
any cyclic conjugated system regardless of electrons
4n pi electrons in the ring system always
non-planar geometry for orbital overlap
